story_title: Belfast anti-immigrant riots erupt after Sudanese man charged with stabbing

sources.role_or_affiliation: Director of the Institute of Irish Studies, University of Liverpool
sources.exact_quote: 'Racists and groups allied to the far right are now unified across Europe'

sources.role_or_affiliation: Sudanese community member, mother of three
sources.exact_quote: “We strongly condemn and reject what happened,”

sources.role_or_affiliation: Sudanese community member, mother of three
sources.exact_quote: “Unfortunately, it turned out that (the suspect) is of Sudanese nationality. But this is the opposite of what is known about our Sudanese people. They are kind people, known for their generosity, their morals, and the way they treat others.”

sources.role_or_affiliation: Sudanese community member, mother of three
sources.exact_quote: “May God reward them with all goodness. We cannot describe what they have done for us,”

sources.role_or_affiliation: Sudanese community member, mother of three
sources.exact_quote: “We feel that not everyone here is unaccepting of foreigners. There is goodness, there are people who love us, people who shared their homes with us, shared our worries, shared our moments of weakness, and took us in.”

sources.role_or_affiliation: Belfast resident whose home was burned
sources.exact_quote: 'I will never get over watching my home burn'
“a lot less than the terrible events that we witnessed on Tuesday night.”
“Above all for those who were intimidated, burned out of their houses by masked thugs on the basis of the color of their skin,”
“the sense of fear that has been created.”
‘People do crimes, we bear the burden’

sources.role_or_affiliation: Author of “If We Tolerate This: How the British Establishment Made the Far Right Acceptable”
sources.exact_quote: I think very often that the specific incidents themselves are local, and related to a national issue, or even something to do with the city or the region that they take place in. What’s changed over the last five to ten years is that the international dimension has become much more significant. Particularly when there is video footage, an event in one country will be taken up by international far-right influencers and networks. And then that feeds far-right narratives and ideas in other countries, but also feeds back into the country where the narrative originated. Certainly that’s the case for the U.K.

sources.role_or_affiliation: Author of “If We Tolerate This: How the British Establishment Made the Far Right Acceptable”
sources.exact_quote: You have to see this rioting as the latest in a string of similar events that have been happening in the U.K., especially in the past two years, and there’s a kind of momentum to them.
